concerns.text: The plan identifies 'NO verified source found' for the primary dataset (PostTags) and proposes a fallback to a 'local copy' or 'public mirror' without a specific, reachable URL. While the Constitution (II. Verified Accuracy) and FR-001 require fetching from a 'canonical source', the plan fails to cite a specific, accessible dataset URL (e.g., a specific archive.org dump ID or data.stackexchange.com export link) in the planning phase. The upstream resolver cannot validate a 'local copy' or a generic 'mirror' claim. The plan must cite a specific, reachable URL for the Stack Overflow data dump (e.g., 'https://archive.org/download/stackexchange/stackoverflow.com-PostsTags.7z') to satisfy the 'Datasets are real' constraint before implementation can proceed.

The plan states for external validation: 'If no verified source for GitHub/NPM data is found... the correlation step will be skipped or performed with a "mock" dataset'. This violates the requirement that datasets must be real and appropriate. A 'mock' dataset cannot validate real-world trends (FR-007). The plan must identify a specific, accessible source for GitHub stars (e.g., GitHub API with rate-limit handling strategy, or a specific Kaggle dataset of historical stars) or NPM downloads (e.g., npm-registry API) to ensure the validation step is feasible with real data, not a placeholder.
